name: Autonomyx Policy Creator
description: Automated website policy generation via Autonomyx Gateway. Input: product description, tech stack, jurisdictions needed. Output: structured JSON with ready-to-publish policy documents (Privacy Policy, Terms of Service, Cookie Policy). Single API call. DPDP 2023, GDPR, CCPA aware. NOTE: For interactive policy creation with sub-processor confirmation, use the website-policy-drafting-skill in Claude.

product_description.placeholder: e.g. B2B SaaS for invoice automation, serving Indian SMEs. Uses Razorpay, Supabase, OpenAI API.

sub_processors.placeholder: Razorpay, Stripe, OpenAI, Supabase, SendGrid, Google Analytics

company_details.placeholder: Company name, registered address, contact email, DPO email if applicable

model_name.info: Qwen3-30B-A3B — strong instruction following, long output, 128k context

max_tokens.info: Policies can be long — 8k tokens for full documents

node.description: Local model — policy content never leaves your VPS
JSON containing all generated policy documents

variables.name: LANGFLOW_VIRTUAL_KEY
variables.description: LiteLLM virtual key for spend tracking

important_notes: This flow is for AUTOMATED policy generation — single call, no interaction
For INTERACTIVE policy creation with sub-processor confirmation, use the website-policy-drafting-skill in Claude
Always have generated policies reviewed by qualified legal counsel before publication
Sub-processors must be explicitly listed — flow will not invent them
DPDP 2023 compliance language included for Indian products by default

api_example.curl:
curl -X POST https://flows.openautonomyx.com/api/v1/run/{flow_id} \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer lf-your-api-key' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"product_name": "InvoiceAI", "product_description": "B2B SaaS for invoice automation. Uses Razorpay and Supabase.", "jurisdictions": "India (DPDP 2023)", "sub_processors": "Razorpay, Supabase", "company_details": "InvoiceAI Pvt Ltd, Bengaluru, hello@invoiceai.com"}'
